reasoning: Timothée Chalamet is heavily favored to win Best Actor for 'Marty Supreme'. He won the Golden Globe for Comedy/Musical and the Critics Choice Award. On Feb 13, William Hill listed his odds at 1/5 (~83% implied probability) and Polymarket had him at 76% a few days earlier. He is also nominated for the BAFTA and presumably the SAG awards (which usually happen in late February). The disparity between his high chances (~80%) and a near 'lock' (>95%) reflects the remaining uncertainty of upcoming major precursor awards (SAG and BAFTA), where competitors like Leonardo DiCaprio or Michael B. Jordan could mount an upset. However, his dominant position across major oddsmakers establishes him as a solid frontrunner. I have adjusted the Polymarket baseline slightly upwards to reflect the latest late-February betting odds (1/5) which show his position remaining secure.
